key_insight: Static equilibrium concepts can hide unstable and chaotic multi-agent dynamics, so worst-case Price of Anarchy guarantees may be misleading or unbounded in several game settings.
engineering_relevance: For distributed systems, routing, and learning-based control, the paper warns that convergence to equilibrium alone is not enough to guarantee robust performance or efficiency.
method: Analyze geometry of Nash and correlated equilibrium sets in relation to learning dynamics
Show instability/repeller behavior in congestion games and positive-affine-cost settings
Study discrete-time learning in non-atomic congestion games and swap-regret dynamics
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical results, including unbounded PoA under strictly positive affine costs, chaotic limit sets under optimal swap-regret minimization, and exponential inefficiency growth as 2^p in non-atomic congestion games.
watch_item: These are worst-case theoretical constructions, so practitioners should be cautious about directly extrapolating the instability and chaos claims to specific real-world systems without model validation.
paper_title: Paradoxes of Game Theoretic Equilibria and Price of Anarchy
